free_text: Statistical gamma decay of the giant dipole resonance
in highly excited 46Ti and 52Cr
A 25.4 cm x 25.4 cm NaI(T1) detector with an
active anticoincidence shield. The central NaI crystal
was surrounded by ~2 cm of 6LiH to absorb thermal
neutrons. The entire spectrometer assembly was
surrounded by 10 cm of Pb shielding on the front
and sides, as well as 36.4 cm of wax in front to
moderate fast neutrons coming from the target.
No information on source of uncertainties.
OG
Target was self-supporting rolled foil of Al metal
The target thickness, as measured by energy loss of
5.486 MeV alpha particles from an 241Am source, was
determined within +/-5% to be 1.39 mg/cm.
Fig.1.
